popularStory.headline: Charles VI (roi de France)
popularStory.description: Charles VI, dit « le Bien-Aimé »1 ou « le Fou »2 comme il a été surnommé au xive siècle (né à Paris, le 3 décembre 1368 - mort à Paris, le 21 octobre 1422) est roi de France de 1380 à 1422. Fils du ro …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Charles Le Fou VI Roi de France於1368年12月3日出生在法國i萊-德-福朗斯芭黎絲時，他的父親Charles V "Le Sage" de Valois Roi de France30歲，母親Jeanne de Bourbon Reine de France30歲。他於1385年7月17日與Isabeau de Bavière在法國皮卡迪阿米安斯結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和6個女兒的父母。他去世於1422年10月21日在他的家鄉享年53歲，埋葬在法國i萊-德-福朗斯瑟-賽吉登尼斯沙吉登尼斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Charles Le Fou VI Roi de France于1368年12月3日出生在法国i萊-德-福朗斯芭黎絲时，他的父亲Charles V "Le Sage" de Valois Roi de France30岁，母亲Jeanne de Bourbon Reine de France30岁。他于1385年7月17日与Isabeau de Bavière在法国皮卡迪阿米安斯结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和6个女儿的父母。他去世于1422年10月21日在他的家乡享年53岁，埋葬在法国i萊-德-福朗斯瑟-賽吉登尼斯沙吉登尼斯。

nameMeanings.name: Charles
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Germanic word, karl, meaning ‘free man’, akin to Old English ceorl ‘man’. The name, Latin form Carolus, owed its popularity in medieval Europe to the Frankish leader Charlemagne ( ?742–814 ), who in 800 established himself as Holy Roman Emperor. His name (Latin Carolus Magnus) means ‘Charles the Great’. Carolus—or Karl, the German form—was a common name among Frankish leaders, including Charlemagne's grandfather Charles Martel ( 688–741 ). Charles is the French form. The name occurs occasionally in medieval Britain as Karolus or Carolus; it had a certain vogue in West Yorkshire from the 1400s, particularly among gentry families. The form Charles was chosen by Mary Queen of Scots ( 1542–87 ), who had been brought up in France, for her son, Charles James ( 1566–1625 ), who became King James VI of Scotland and, from 1603 , James I of England. His son and grandson both reigned as King Charles , and the name thus became established in the 17th century both in the Stuart royal house and among English and Scottish supporters of the Stuart monarchy. In the 18th century it was to some extent favoured, along with James , by Jacobites, supporters of the exiled Stuarts, opposed to the Hanoverian monarchy, especially in the Highlands of Scotland. In the 19th century the popularity of the name was further enhanced by romanticization of the story of ‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’, leader of the 1745 rebellion.

nameMeanings.name: Le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

nameMeanings.name: Fou
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Generally a transferred use of the surname, which derives as a nickname from the animal (Old English fox). This may originally have been given to a cunning person, or to someone with red hair, or for some other anecdotal reason. Use as a given name may have originated in honour of George Fox ( 1624–91 ), founder of the Quaker movement. In Ireland it has also been used as an English translation of the Gaelic nickname Sionnach ‘fox’. As a Jewish name it has been used as an Anglicized form of the Yiddish nickname fiksl ‘fox’.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Charles Le Fou VI Roi de France was born on 3 December 1368, in Paris, Île-de-France, France, his father, Charles V "Le Sage" de Valois Roi de France, was 30 and his mother, Jeanne de Bourbon Reine de France, was 30. He married Isabeau de Bavière on 17 July 1385, in Amiens, Picardie, France. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 6 daughters. He died on 21 October 1422, in his hometown, at the age of 53, and was buried in Saint-Denis, Seine-Saint-Denis, Île-de-France, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Charles Le Fou VI Roi de France est né le 3 décembre 1368, à Paris, Île-de-France, France, son père, Charles V "Le Sage" de Valois Roi de France, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Jeanne de Bourbon Reine de France, avait 30 ans. Il a épousé Isabeau de Bavière le 17 juillet 1385, à Amiens, Picardie, France. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 6 filles. Il est décédé le 21 octobre 1422, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 53 ans, et a été enterré à Saint-Denis, Seine-Saint-Denis, Île-de-France, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Charles Le Fou VI Roi de France am 3. Dezember 1368 in Paris, Île-de-France, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Charles V "Le Sage" de Valois Roi de France 30 und seine Mutter Jeanne de Bourbon Reine de France 30. Er hat am 17. Juli 1385 in Amiens, Picardie, Frankreich Isabeau de Bavière geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Er starb am 21. Oktober 1422 im Alter von 53 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Saint-Denis, Seine-Saint-Denis, Île-de-France, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Charles Le Fou VI Roi de France è nato il il 3 dicembre 1368 nel Paris, Île-de-France, Francia, il suo padre, Charles V "Le Sage" de Valois Roi de France, aveva 30 anni e il suo madre, Jeanne de Bourbon Reine de France, aveva 30 anni. Lui sposato Isabeau de Bavière il 17 luglio 1385, nel Amiens, Picardie, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 6 figlie. Morì il 21 ottobre 1422, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 53 anni, e fu sepolto a Saint-Denis, Seine-Saint-Denis, Île-de-France, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Charles Le Fou VI Roi de Franceが1368年12月3日にフランスiレ-デ-フランスパーイスで生まれた時，父Charles V "Le Sage" de Valois Roi de Franceは30歳，母Jeanne de Bourbon Reine de Franceは30歳であった。1385年7月17日にフランスピカーディアミエンスでIsabeau de Bavièreと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼は1422年10月21日に故郷で53歳で死去し，フランスiレ-デ-フランスセアイネ-サイント-デニスサイント-デニスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Charles Le Fou VI Roi de France(이)가 1368년 12월 3일 프랑스 i레-데-프랜스 파리스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Charles V "Le Sage" de Valois Roi de France(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Jeanne de Bourbon Reine de France(은)는 30세였다. 그는 1385년 7월 17일 프랑스 피카디 아미스에서 Isabeau de Bavière(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1422년 10월 21일 그의 고향에서 53세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 i레-데-프랜스 세이인-세인트-데니스 세인트-데니스에 묻혔다.

mn.3 December 1368–21 October 1422: 1368 оны Арванхоёрдугаар сарын 3–1422 оны Аравдугаар сарын 21

pt.3 December 1368–21 October 1422: 3 de dezembro de 1368–21 de outubro de 1422
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Charles Le Fou VI Roi de France nasceu em 3 de dezembro de 1368, em Paris, Île-de-France, França, seu pai, Charles V "Le Sage" de Valois Roi de France, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Jeanne de Bourbon Reine de France, tinha 30 anos. Ele casou-se com Isabeau de Bavière em 17 de julho de 1385, em Amiens, Picardie, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 6 filhas. Ele faleceu em 21 de outubro de 1422, em sua cidade natal, com 53 anos, e foi sepultado em Saint-Denis, Seine-Saint-Denis, Île-de-France, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Charles Le Fou VI Roi de France родился 3 декабря 1368 г. в местности пэрис, iле-де-фрэнс, Франция, его отцу по имени Charles V "Le Sage" de Valois Roi de France было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Jeanne de Bourbon Reine de France, 30. Он женился на женщине по имени Isabeau de Bavière 17 июля 1385 г. в местности амиенс, пикарди, Франция. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 6 дочерей. Он умер 21 октября 1422 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 53 лет и был похоронен в местности сеинт-денис, сине-сеинт-денис, iле-де-фрэнс, Франция.

es.3 December 1368–21 October 1422: 3 de diciembre de 1368–21 de octubre de 1422
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Charles Le Fou VI Roi de France nació el 3 de diciembre de 1368, en Paris, Île-de-France, Francia, su papá, Charles V "Le Sage" de Valois Roi de France, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Jeanne de Bourbon Reine de France, tenía 30 años. Se casó con Isabeau de Bavière el 17 de julio de 1385, en Amiens, Picardie, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 6 hijas. Murió el 21 de octubre de 1422, en su pueblo, a la edad de 53 años, y fue sepultado en Saint-Denis, Seine-Saint-Denis, Île-de-France, Francia.
